institution: Nile University — Faculty of Information Technology and Computer Science
methodology_note: Areas of strength and weakness within the internal environment were identified and diagnosed for each criterion drawn from the quality standards, as follows.

strengths: The Faculty has an approved and announced vision and mission
The Faculty has published its vision and mission through multiple channels
Faculty management supports the philosophy of quality and continuous improvement
Mechanisms have been put in place to measure performance efficiency in all areas
Communication is available via the information network, allowing various parties from outside the Faculty to participate in the environmental analysis
weaknesses: Lack of sufficient collective awareness of the importance of strategic planning
Limited willingness among students and community parties to participate in opinion surveys

strengths: Approved and announced criteria exist for selecting academic leadership
Existence of an Advisory Board for the Faculty
A handbook exists for faculty members that serves as an announced and approved work charter, with mechanisms to ensure transparency, fair practices, and non-discrimination among Faculty members
The organizational structure is suitable for the Faculty's size and activity, with flexibility to develop it by adding new elements to meet increasing requirements
Management is keen to take on board the views and suggestions of faculty members
Effective mechanisms exist for communicating with students and resolving their problems
Program councils are actively activated
Faculty management has adopted an open-door policy for resolving issues
weaknesses: Weak training plan for academic leadership
Absence of Vice Dean positions at the Faculty
Absence of a guide on intellectual property rights and professional ethics
Weak awareness of the tasks required of each member within the system

strengths: A Quality Assurance Unit exists at the Faculty with internal regulations, a work plan, and databases for its activities
Coordination and cooperation take place with the university's Quality Unit
Faculty members, supporting staff, and the administrative apparatus participate in quality work
Self-assessment is conducted through the Faculty's annual report
Student survey results are analyzed periodically using modern techniques
Evaluation mechanisms are leveraged in areas of improvement and development
The satisfaction level of beneficiaries of the Faculty's services is measured regularly
Performance evaluation criteria are announced, clear, and fair
An improvement plan exists to raise the quality of the educational process
weaknesses: Delay in appointing personnel to the Unit's administrative structure
Unsuitability of the space allocated to the Unit
Absence of mechanisms for internal and external review
Slow responsiveness in completing quality work due to the heavy teaching loads and other duties assigned to faculty members

strengths: The scientific specialization of faculty members is suitable for the courses they teach
Procedures exist for handling shortages and surpluses among faculty members and supporting staff
The ratio of faculty members and supporting staff to students at the Faculty level is consistent with benchmark rates
Mechanisms exist to survey the opinions of faculty members and supporting staff and to take the necessary actions to improve them
Objective criteria and clear mechanisms exist to evaluate the performance of faculty members and supporting staff, notify them of the results, and discuss them to improve performance
Attracting experts from industry and global companies to participate in teaching some specialized courses
Clear mechanisms exist for appointing faculty members and supporting staff
Appointment of distinguished personnel to the supporting staff
weaknesses: A large number of part-time faculty members
High turnover rate among faculty members
Insufficient training plan for developing faculty members' capabilities and skills
Absence of some specializations the Faculty wishes to expand into
Absence of a consultancy and training center at the Faculty

strengths: The university provides central administrations serving the Faculty and other faculties
Long-serving staff at the Faculty are proficient in using information-technology tools, enabling them to raise the level of internal performance
Mechanisms exist to survey staff opinions
An outstanding recruitment system exists based on a specific skills-defined job description, applied precisely to attract talent
An appropriate salary and incentive system exists to attract distinguished cadres
Suitable workspaces and the availability of appropriate equipment for them
weaknesses: Weak familiarity of the university's administrative team with accreditation and quality standards
Weak experience of newly recruited personnel in academic work areas due to rapid staff turnover across different administrations
The number of administrative staff is not keeping pace with the growing number of students
Need for additional personnel in the administrative apparatus for documentation and announcement purposes
Staff surveys for evaluating training courses are insufficiently completed

strengths: Being a non-profit university leads to using all material resources to improve performance and cover any deficit
Self-generated resources vary between student fees and research projects funded domestically or internationally
A growing number of undergraduate and Master's students
Availability of lecture halls, labs, and seminar/conference rooms for carrying out educational, research, and community-service activities
Strong infrastructure, good information systems, and internet connectivity
Teaching halls are suitable for the educational process (in terms of lighting, ventilation, audio equipment, and display screens/devices)
A modern library suitable for the university's educational and research requirements
Buildings suitable for people with special needs
Very good cleanliness and regular maintenance
weaknesses: Absence of a scientific and technical consultancy center
Insufficient specialized labs at the Faculty for scientific research
Absence of accommodation for relocating/international students and supporting staff
Absence of dedicated space for the Quality Unit
Need for more space for future expansions

strengths: Academic standards are adopted from comparable local and international programs, plus the guiding standards issued by the National Authority for Quality Assurance and Accreditation of Education
The active educational programs are suitable for labor-market needs
Clear, documented, and announced descriptions exist for educational programs and courses
Matrices exist mapping courses against the adopted academic standards
Alignment of the design of the Faculty's educational programs with the Faculty's mission
Establishment of new programs that keep pace with the evolving needs of the labor market
weaknesses: Need to redescribe programs based on competencies and apply this in the reference academic standards for the Faculty's programs

strengths: Multiple teaching and learning strategies are adopted to suit various learning styles in order to achieve the targeted learning outcomes
Support for interactive learning based on project work within courses, which helps graduate cadres capable of problem-solving and teamwork
Encouraging cooperation protocols with universities of advanced academic ranking
Effective academic advising for student awareness, information, and guidance
Mechanisms exist to document exam results and announce them to students electronically
Diversity in student-assessment methods to achieve continuous and fair measurement of student achievement
Faculty halls and labs are equipped to conduct electronic exams for students
A system exists for handling and following up on student grievances
Continuous analysis of student results and corrective actions taken to ensure good achievement
The Faculty has an approved and documented teaching and learning strategy
The teaching and learning strategy is continuously reviewed and the review results are utilized
weaknesses: Some imbalance in distributing academic workload on students across the semester
Limited academic support provided to students with special needs
Need for more cooperation with companies to support graduation projects

strengths: Faculty graduates possess outstanding informational and practical foundations
The Faculty's website contains all information related to admission, transfers, course schedules, activities, seminars, the library, and results
A system exists for handling and following up on student grievances
Electronic publication of numerous courses and labs
The relationship between faculty members and students is characterized by constant communication, whether in person or via various communication technologies
Electronic surveys of student opinion on courses, complaints, and suggestions
The Faculty is keen on the role of the Student Union and works to activate its contributions in the field of student activities
Offering a number of full and partial scholarships for distinguished students and providing financial support to students
Availability of material resources to support students' health, social, and sports needs
Encouraging participation in international and local scientific competitions
The educational system is based on integrating skills into learning outcomes
weaknesses: Absence of an objective system for admitting students and distributing them among programs when the number of applicants to a program exceeds the permitted limit
Weak mechanisms for following up and communicating with undergraduate graduates during the years following graduation

strengths: A documented and approved scientific research plan exists, linked to the university's plan and to national directions and the needs of the surrounding community
Existence of prominent scientific figures among researchers across various research fields
Availability of material support for researchers and support for scientific publication at conferences and in scientific journals
Existence of some research projects funded by local and international research institutions
Some faculty members participate in reviewing international research papers and projects
A clear mechanism exists for evaluating the research activity of faculty members
Existence of a specialized center to support researchers
Encouragement of forming research groups
Encouraging competitive bids to obtain funding for research projects and local/international grants
Availability of scientific books, references, periodicals, and theses at the library
weaknesses: Need for more long-term partnerships, funded and planned with national institutions and applied-research centers
Weak awareness of how to obtain patents, how to market them, and methods for protecting and licensing software intellectual-property rights

strengths: Graduate-study tracks are aligned with actual market needs
Serious follow-up on academic supervision and progress in scientific research
Alignment of the faculty member's specialization and experience with the subject of the thesis they supervise
The Faculty's regulations allow graduates from all specializations to enroll in graduate studies, provided they demonstrate seriousness and capability
Encouraging graduate students to publish research extracted from theses and coursework
weaknesses: Difficulty offering a large number of courses suited to students' choices due to economic cost
Limited number of places allowed for graduate students

strengths: Participation of representatives from the local community on the Faculty's Advisory Board and in various Faculty activities
Support for participation in diverse student activities directed at community service and developing the environment surrounding the Faculty
Organizing numerous seminars, meetings, and events within the Faculty
Existence of a distinguished set of technical diplomas suited to community needs
Existence of the Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Competitiveness Center
weaknesses: Absence of dedicated centers for community service such as technological training
Absence of dedicated centers for providing expert consultancy
